subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: The 19th century is known as the period of 'Indian Renaissance'
The establishment and reforms of Brahma Samaj, Arya Samaj, Prathana Samaj, Satyashodhaka Samaj will be known
The Young Bengali and Aligarh Movements will be explained
The teachings of Ramakrishna Mission will be understood
The ideas of Narayana Guru's Dharmaparipalana Yogam and Periyar will be understood

key_terms.definition: The 19th century period of social and religious reform awakening in India
Social reform organization founded by Raja Ram Mohan Roy in 1828
Reform organization founded by Dayanand Saraswati with the slogan 'Back to the Vedas'
Social reform organization founded by Jyotiba Phule against caste discrimination
Movement started by Arya Samaj for reconversion of people who had left Hinduism
Muslim reform movement started by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan to promote modern education

mcqs.question: The social reform organization based on the principle 'service to humanity is service to God'
mcqs.explanation: Ramakrishna Mission founded by Swami Vivekananda was based on this principle

mcqs.question: The Theosophical Society was founded by
mcqs.explanation: The Theosophical Society was founded in 1875 by Colonel H.S. Olcott and Madam Blavatsky

mcqs.question: The Call 'Back to the Vedas' was given by
mcqs.explanation: Dayanand Saraswati, founder of Arya Samaj, gave the call 'Back to the Vedas'

mcqs.question: The newspaper 'New India' was started by
mcqs.explanation: Annie Besant started the newspaper 'New India'

mcqs.question: 'Purification Ritual' was the main activity of
mcqs.explanation: Arya Samaj conducted Shuddhi (purification) ritual ceremonies

one_mark_questions.question: How is the 19th century in Indian history called?
Who started the Self Respect Movement?
Who was the founder of the Prarthana Samaj?
Who was the teacher of Swami Vivekananda?
Who started the Young Bengali Movement?
Who started the Anglo Oriental College?
Who said 'There is one caste, one religion and one God for the human race'?

three_mark_questions.question: State the reforms of Dayananda Saraswati / Objectives of Arya Samaj.
answer: Said that the Vedas are the source of truth and knowledge
Condemned idol worship and caste system
Called for the use of swadeshi goods

three_mark_questions.question: Analyse the objectives of the Aligarh Movement.
answer: Promoting harmony of Eastern and Western ideas
Transformation of Muslims in their political, social, educational, and religious philosophical beliefs
Sir Syed Ahmed Khan started Anglo-Oriental College to provide modern education to Muslims
Providing religious education along with modern education
Creating a modern society through western education
Supported female literacy and condemned polygamy and ideas against widow marriage
